threat_model: MAF ABI/CLI contract evidence can accidentally become a runtime execution claim if a scaffold-only CLI surface is conflated with command implementation, subprocess execution, PyO3 binding, or Python-to-Rust runtime binding. The mitigation is a read-only witness and validator that reject authority drift, scaffold drift, digest drift, command execution drift, raw secret markers, receipt ref drift, and summary drift.

required_checks.check: CLI execution, subprocess execution, PyO3 binding, Rust crate execution, Python Rust imports, runtime dispatch, canonical state mutation, terminal closure, and success authority remain false
external connector calls and network calls remain denied by the authority boundary
redaction check confirms secret marker validation rejects client_secret, tokens, API keys, and private-key markers in witness payloads
policy bypass check confirms Foundation Mode contract mode remains scaffold_only_static_cli_contract and command behavior remains AwaitingEvidence

receipt integrity check confirms receipt refs bind the witness schema, example, validator, tests, MAF parity witness, MAF boundary doc, receipt coverage doc, UAO schema, LifeMeaningJudgment schema, and WorkerFailureReceipt schema
audit visibility check confirms CLI artifact digests, command counts, authority denial counts, and gap counts cannot drift

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode witness blocks CLI execution, subprocess execution, PyO3 binding, Rust crate execution, Python Rust imports, runtime dispatch, terminal closure, and success claims.
Witness records scaffold-only CLI status and requires command contracts to remain AwaitingEvidence with execution_allowed false.

mitigations: keep MafAbiCliContractWitness validation read-only and deterministic
require canonical source digests for MAF CLI artifacts
require open gap refs for every command contract
deny CLI, subprocess, PyO3, Rust execution, connectors, network, secrets, writes, dispatch, terminal closure, and success claims
reject secret markers and summary count drift
